scope: Review the promoted Initiative 120 Space design meta-scope, the Research Observatory profile prototype, DesignRealitySnapshotV1, DesignAuditUnitV1, and the local NDL profile linter. Determine whether the work correctly treats current design/theme contracts as locked truth and whether Hermes has enough bounded input to provide recommendations for Space-level design standards, templates, and imported design elements without approving, mutating, or enforcing anything.
requiredQuestions: Does the plan correctly treat current NDL, A2UI, brand, accessibility, ViewSpec, Cortex Web, Space capability, and Hermes boundaries as locked design reality?
Does NdlDesignProfileV1 contain the right authority metadata for Space-level design profiles, or should it be renamed/promoted to SpaceDesignProfileV1 first?
What is missing before DesignElementImportV1 and SpaceTemplatePackV1 can be safely introduced?
Does the local linter enforce the right first-pass safety checks?
Are there contradictions or drift between Initiative 120, Initiative 074, Initiative 115, Initiative 123, Initiative 130, Initiative 132, shared branding, and accessibility standards?
What should the next recommendation-only implementation slice be?
governanceNotes: Hermes must remain read-only and recommendation-only. It must not edit files, approve profiles, import design systems, mutate runtime state, activate skills, or treat request-worktree paths as authority. Use only promoted root ICP paths and local Hermes guardrails.
